name: Real Estate Education And Community Housing
description_short: HUD-approved nonprofit providing free homebuyer education, foreclosure counseling, and down payment assistance to low-to-moderate income families in Florida.
description_long:
Real Estate Education and Community Housing (R.E.A.C.H.) is a Florida-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization founded in January 2006. The organization is HUD-approved and serves as a Foreclosure and Housing Counseling Agency, operating as an affiliate of HomeFree-USA. R.E.A.C.H.'s stated mission is to inspire and motivate low-to-moderate income community members to achieve homeownership through education, counseling, and grant assistance.

R.E.A.C.H. offers multiple programs designed to support homeownership and financial stability. Their core services include HUD-approved homebuyer education workshops using NeighborWorks curriculum, foreclosure and eviction prevention counseling, pre-purchase counseling, down payment assistance programs, and credit mitigation services. They provide post-closing home maintenance classes approved by the City of West Palm Beach, and offer programs like eHome America and Save My Home for foreclosure education and prevention. Services are delivered in multiple languages whenever possible.

The organization distinguishes itself through partnerships with local mortgage lenders, real estate professionals, and attorneys who facilitate workshops and provide practical guidance to participants. R.E.A.C.H. offers specialized credit mitigation programming with access to Second Chance Grants for qualifying individuals to pay off past-due accounts. Their programs are designed in conjunction with governmental and private agencies, and they operate with transparent fee structures and donation-supported operations.

As a genuinely nonprofit, free-help provider, R.E.A.C.H. serves families who may otherwise lack access to homeownership education and foreclosure prevention. The main limitation is geographic scope—services appear concentrated in Florida, particularly the Palm Beach County area. The organization's reliance on grants and donations means program availability may fluctuate with funding.
pros: HUD-approved nonprofit with transparent 501(c)(3) status and no profit motive
NeighborWorks-accredited homebuyer education curriculum meeting Palm Beach County standards
Multilingual program delivery in the native language of participants whenever possible
Free or low-cost services including counseling, workshops, and down payment assistance guidance
Specialized foreclosure prevention counseling with experienced home ownership counselors
Second Chance Grant program available to pay off past-due accounts for qualifying individuals
Direct facilitation with local mortgage lenders and real estate professionals in workshops
cons: Geographic limitation—services appear primarily available in Florida, particularly Palm Beach County area
Website navigation and information hierarchy could be clearer; some program details are scattered
Funding dependent on donations and grants, which may create program availability uncertainty
Limited information about typical counseling timeframes, wait times, or case completion rates
No clear eligibility income thresholds specified on website despite 'low-moderate income' focus
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Florida seeking free education and financial guidance before purchase
Low-to-moderate income families facing foreclosure seeking HUD-approved prevention counseling
Prospective homeowners needing down payment assistance programs and credit counseling
Non-English speakers seeking multilingual homebuyer education and housing counseling
diagnosis: R.E.A.C.H. is best for Florida-based, low-to-moderate income first-time homebuyers and homeowners facing foreclosure who need free, HUD-approved counseling and education. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to Florida and program availability dependent on nonprofit funding; prospective clients outside Florida or in other states will need to seek alternative HUD-approved housing counseling agencies.
services: Free 8-hour homebuyer education workshops (virtual and in-person)
HUD-approved foreclosure prevention and counseling services
Down payment assistance program guidance and qualification support
Credit mitigation and financial wellness education
Second Chance Grant program for past-due accounts
HELOC (home equity line of credit) education
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1451 W Cypress Creek Road, Suite 391, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33309
